name: Thrones (Ophanim)
shortDescription: Thrones, also known as Ophanim, are celestial beings in Christian angelology, associated with God's justice and authority.
description: Thrones, or Ophanim (from the Hebrew for 'wheels'), are a class of angels within Christian angelology, often associated with judgment, authority, and divine justice. Their appearance is frequently described as wheels within wheels, covered in eyes, symbolizing God's all-seeing nature and the execution of His will. They are said to carry God's throne, hence their name. The Book of Ezekiel provides a key source for understanding their imagery, depicting them as part of God's chariot. In some interpretations, they are associated with the Cherubim and Seraphim as among the highest orders of angels. Their role is to uphold God's throne and ensure the divine will is carried out. They are considered powerful and awe-inspiring beings, embodying divine justice and the unwavering execution of God's commands. Their ceaseless spinning represents the constant motion and omnipresence of God's power and judgment throughout creation. Some scholars also link them to the celestial spheres, representing the movement of the planets and stars, further emphasizing their connection to the divine order of the cosmos.
appearance: Wheels within wheels, covered in eyes
